key_insight: The paper derives exact worst- and best-case distorted utility values under moment ambiguity by solving the inner distributional problem in the quantile domain with nonsmooth optimality conditions and isotonic projection.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical oracle for robust decision-making in pricing, inventory, contracts, and capacity planning when only low-order moments are known, improving protection against tail risk.
method: Reformulate the moment-based inner problem in quantile space.
Use nonsmooth variational analysis to obtain exact first-order conditions and closed-form extremal values/distributions.
Handle monotonicity via isotonic projection onto the monotone cone for a scalable linear-time inner solve.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports three recovered/extended classical examples and a real capacity-provisioning case for generative AI inference, where ambiguity-aware robust optimization reduced required capacity while maintaining service compliance.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate moment estimates and a discretized isotonic projection step, so performance may degrade if the supplied moments are noisy or the quantile discretization is too coarse.
paper_title: Extreme-Case Distorted Utility under Moment Ambiguity
